data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration:

1. **Project file changes:**
   - `xunit` v2.9.3 → `xunit.v3` (latest v3 package)
   - `xunit.runner.visualstudio` → `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3 or the new runner
   - `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` - might still be needed or might change
   - `Xunit.Combinatorial` → need to check v3 compatibility
   - `Xunit.StaFact` → need to check v3 compatibility
   - Target framework needs to be at least net6.0 (net8.0 works fine), and the project output type must be changed to `Exe` for v3

2. **Code changes:**
   - The using statements should remain largely the same, though the namespace might shift to `Xunit.v3`
   - Test classes are already public which is required
   - `[Fact]` and `[Theory]` attributes continue to work as before
   - `[CombinatorialData]` comes from the `Xunit.Combinatorial` package
   - `[StaFact]` comes from the `Xunit.StaFact` package

Now I'm thinking through the actual package and namespace changes for v3. The main shift is moving from the `xunit` package to `xunit.v3`, and the test runner also needs updating. The project structure requires that executable output type, and I need to verify what the namespace imports should be since there might be some changes there beyond just the basic attributes.

For the third-party packages like `Xunit.Combinatorial` and `Xunit.StaFact`, I'll need to check whether v3-compatible versions exist or if there are alternatives. I need to verify whether `Xunit.Combinatorial` and `Xunit.StaFact` have v3-compatible versions available on NuGet, since the extensibility model changed significantly between v2 and v3 and third-party packages won't work without updates.

Let me focus on the core xUnit v3 migration steps: switching from the `xunit` package to `xunit.v3`, updating the test runner configuration, adding the executable output type, and adjusting the global.json settings accordingly. I need to figure out the right configuration for xUnit v3 with `dotnet test` support, which means keeping `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and setting the output type to executable. For the global.json, xUnit v3 works with `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` as the runner, so I should update that configuration and then try building to see what compatibility issues arise with the third-party packages.

Run your xunit-based tests on an STA thread with the WPF Dispatcher, a WinForms SynchronizationContext, or even a cross-platform generic UI thread emulation with a SynchronizationContext that keeps code running on a "main thread" for that test.

Simply use `[WpfFact]`, `[WinFormsFact]`, `[StaFact]` or the cross-platform `[UIFact]` on your test method to run your test under conditions that most closely match the main thread in your application.

Theory variants of these attributes allow for parameterized testing. Check out the xunit.combinatorial NuGet package for pairwise or combinatorial testing with theories.

Use v1.x versions of this package for use with xUnit v2. Use v2.0 versions of this package for use with xUnit.v3 v1.x. Use v2.1+ versions of this package for use with xUnit.v3 v2+. Use v3.0+ versions of this package for use with xunit.v3 v3+

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._

See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._
